articles.headline: Do Airlines Make Money From Economy Seats?
articles.blurb: This heavily depends on the carrier and its operations.
articles.takeaway: Economy seat profitability varies significantly by airline based on cost structure, load factors, and ancillary revenue strategies rather than base fares alone.

articles.headline: Alaska Airlines Pilots Ask For Police “To Meet The Airplane” After Passenger Vapes Onboard
articles.blurb: An Alaska Airlines flight from Seattle to Sacramento had to request police to meet the aircraft at the gate after a passenger allegedly vaped.
articles.takeaway: Crew requests for police intervention over in-flight vaping incidents signal escalating safety and security concerns that may prompt stricter enforcement protocols across carriers.

articles.headline: SMS Declaration of Compliance

articles.headline: I’m A Flight Attendant: This Is Why Boarding Is The Most Stressful Part Of The Job

articles.blurb: The flying supercomputers of 2026.

articles.headline: Why Do Airlines Keep Older Aircraft In Service Longer Than Expected?
articles.blurb: Older aircraft come with higher operating costs, but have unique capabilities and have already been paid off by their operator.
articles.takeaway: Fully depreciated aircraft generate positive cash flow despite maintenance costs, making them economically rational to operate until replacement aircraft arrive during supply chain constraints.

articles.headline: The Evolution of Aviation SMS
articles.blurb: From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.
articles.takeaway: Modern SMS frameworks shift aviation from investigating accidents after they occur to systematically preventing hazards before they materialize.

articles.headline: Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization
articles.blurb: Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.
articles.takeaway: Operational optimization across all aviation domains offers immediate emissions reductions without waiting for next-generation aircraft, presenting a near-term sustainability advantage for airlines and airports.
keywords: aviation; optimization; operations research; sustainability; environmental impacts

articles.headline: NASA Selects Intuitive Machines to Deliver Artemis Science, Tech to Moon

articles.headline: ESA to decide by June on Europe’s Gateway contributions

articles.takeaway: NASA's Gateway halt creates immediate budget and partnership uncertainty for ESA, forcing European space agencies to reassess lunar program commitments within three months.

articles.headline: Gallery: Luke Days 2026

articles.headline: NISAR’s View of Mount Rainier
articles.blurb: Description This image captured by U.S.-Indian Earth satellite NISAR on Nov. 10, 2025, shows Washington’s Mount Rainier. The image is cropped from a much larger swath spanning the Pacific Northwest on a cloudy day; NISAR’s L-band SAR instrument is able to peer through the clouds at the surface...
articles.takeaway: NISAR's L-band synthetic aperture radar enables all-weather Earth observation independent of cloud cover, expanding persistent monitoring capabilities for geological and environmental surveillance.

articles.headline: NTSB Issues Preliminary Report on Hudson River Ditching

articles.takeaway: Loss of oil pressure as a precursor to complete engine failure highlights the critical importance of real-time engine monitoring systems and pre-flight oil inspection protocols in preventing catastrophic in-flight emergencies.

articles.headline: NASA Tech and Science Bound for Low Earth Orbit on Commercial Launch
